mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up your cluster resources and persistent volumes, migrate resources between clusters, and replicate production environments for testing. It is particularly useful for disaster recovery and data migration scenarios. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of storage backends and potential complexity in managing backups and restores.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This is the official repository for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Velero to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

```

> This installs the necessary CRDs for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Velero in the specified namespace with the necessary configuration.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-velero-bucket' with your actual S3 bucket name.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

steps.title: Verify Velero installation
steps.description:
This step checks if Velero is installed and running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero server pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step removes Velero and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all backups and data associated with Velero.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command will remove the Velero release.
> This command will delete the Velero namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current Velero configuration. This step upgrades Velero to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get backups.velero.io --all-namespaces

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> List all backups to ensure you have a record before upgrading.
> This command upgrades Velero to version 1.18.0.
> Verify that the Velero pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup: no valid backup storage location found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Velero cannot find a valid backup storage location.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the backup storage location is correctly configured in the Velero installation.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'The server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRDs are not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Backup failed: unable to access the S3 bucket'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a misconfiguration in the S3 bucket settings or permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the S3 bucket permissions and ensure that the IAM role used by Velero has access to the bucket.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Volume snapshot location not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified volume snapshot location is not configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the volume snapshot location is correctly set in the Velero configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for additional cloud providers, improved backup performance, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old backup storage location configuration format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17, ensure to update your backup storage location configuration to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero server pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing: kubectl get pods -n velero.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
